channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A wave of Trump-demanded departures hits senior leadership at the State Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A large number of senior career diplomats who served in politically appointed top leadership positions at the State Department have left their posts at the demand of the incoming Trump administration, which plans to install its own people in those positions, according to current an
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump returns to power after unprecedented comeback, emboldened to reshape American institutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th president Monday, promising a “revolution of common sense” and taking charge as Republicans assume unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country’s institutions.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump declares ‘Golden Age’ in America in inaugural address
item.description: President Trump on Monday declared it was the beginning of a new “Golden Age" in America, pledging to restore a country that he said had been decimated by the previous administration as he officially took the oath of office for his second White House term.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI acting chief says he's retiring as Trump's pick to lead the bureau awaits Senate confirmation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Paul Abbate, who became the FBI's acting director following the departure of Christopher Wray, is retiring from the bureau, according to an email he sent to senior executives that was obtained by The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inauguration Day Latest: Trump moves swiftly to implement new agenda, Biden departs Capitol
item.description: Donald Trump has been sworn in as the 47th President of the United States, taking charge as Republicans claim unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country’s institutions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Melania dons hat at Trump inauguration, struggles to kiss Trump
item.description: First lady Melania Trump wore a navy and ivory ensemble by American designer Adam Lippes for the inauguration ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Leonard Peltier granted clemency by President Biden
item.description: The outgoing administration has released Leonard Peltier from prison. Numerous activists and tribal officials have requested the release of Peltier, whom they believe to be innocent of killing two FBI agents in 1975.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera and Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Michelle Obama skips Trump inauguration as Barack arrives solo
item.description: Former President Barack Obama arrived at the U.S. Capitol on Monday for President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony without former First Lady Michelle Obama.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside Trump's Inauguration Day: How, when and what to watch
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will take the oath of office at noon followed by a presidential parade, signing ceremony and inaugural balls.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Trump will issue executive orders to remake immigration policies but they will face challenges
item.description: The Trump administration is ending use of a border app called CBP One that has allowed nearly 1 million people to legally enter the United States with eligibility to work.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon And Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump expected to sign record number of executive orders on Monday
item.description: "We are going to sign them at the beginning … we are doing them tomorrow,” Trump said at a Sunday rally.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Live updates: Trump holds victory rally at Capital One Arena, TikTok restores service
item.description: President-elect Trump made the rounds rounds in Washington on Sunday and held a victory rally at Capital One Area just before Inauguration Day. Trump arrived in the nation's capital over the weekend where he is preparing to take a second term in the White House as the Republican trifecta takes form.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Capitol Rotunda prepped as inaugural ceremony moves indoors
item.description: The decision Friday to move the ceremony inside forced organizers and security agencies to re-do hundreds of decisions meticulously laid out for months.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most voters in new survey seek toned-down Trump agenda
item.description: Over 60% of Americans polled disagree with Trump's idea to replace thousands of career civil-service workers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok says it's restoring service to U.S. users based on Trump's promised executive order
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he plans to issue an executive order that would give TikTok’s China-based parent company more time to find an approved buyer before the popular video-sharing platform is subject to a permanent ban in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Why Trump's tariff promises will be hard to keep
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said his tariffs will raise revenues, boost U.S. jobs, and help stop the drug trade. But some of these goals are at odds with the others.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Steve Bannon says MAGA populism will win
item.description: Former Trump adviser Steve Bannon says he doesn't believe the president-elect will listen to "tech oligarchs" over the right-wing populist movement that helped him secure another White House term.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: TikTok is offline in the U.S. after Supreme Court upholds ban
item.description: The app had more than 170 million monthly users in the U.S. The black-out is the result of a law forcing the service offline unless it sheds its ties to ByteDance, its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Thousands march in anti-Trump protests just days before former president returns to power
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul on Saturday afternoon, braving single-digit temperatures to protest ahead of President-elect Donald Trump inauguration. Trump takes the oath of office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Feven Gerezgiher and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: After major 2024 defeats, the Democratic Party searches for a new direction
item.description: The Democratic Party begins 2025 with several looming questions. Among them: who will lead its national party apparatus, and how it will handle President-elect Donald Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court upholds law banning TikTok if it’s not sold by its Chinese parent company
item.description: The Supreme Court has unanimously upheld the federal law banning TikTok beginning Sunday unless it’s sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden sets record by commuting sentences of nearly 2,500 people convicted on nonviolent drug charges
item.description: President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of almost 2,500 people convicted of nonviolent drug offenses. Friday’s clemency actions give Biden the presidential record for most individual pardons and commutations issued.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump, New York City Mayor Eric Adams to meet at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams is meeting President-elect Donald Trump on Friday at Mar-a-Lago, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Gavel in or gavel out? DeSantis calls for amendment reform
item.description: There are still a lot of questions about Governor Ron DeSantis' call for a special session in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis outlines immigration plan for lawmakers to consider ahead of special session
item.description: During a stop at the Polk County Sheriff’s Office, Gov. Ron DeSantis proposed a list of statewide immigration measures ahead of a special session.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas 'Preview Mania 2025' set for Thursday with a nod to WWE
item.description: About 1,500 business leaders are expected to attend Las Vegas Preview on Thursday at the Las Vegas Convention Center to hear about the trends that are shaping economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
